title: Echo Pattern
story: [Meridian-7 Log #004] Strange repeating patterns have appeared in the signal. In some data lines, the same word appears consecutively twice. This isn't a transmission error — rEx seems to use repetition to emphasize key information, like how humans use bold text. Find these "echoes".
promptChallenge: Write a prompt to have AI generate a regex that matches lines containing consecutive repeated words
hints: [Hint] Repetition means: the same word, appearing twice, right next to each other
[Hint] Regex has a way to "remember" previously matched content
[Hint] Think about word boundaries and backreferences
promptHints: Tell the AI you're looking for "the same word appearing twice consecutively"
Hint the AI to use backreferences
Describe that words are separated by spaces

feedback.onPass: [Echo Resolved] Repetition isn't noise. rEx is emphasizing something...
feedback.onFail: [Parse Failed] Echo not captured. The repeating structure is more precise than you think.
feedback.onPerfect: [Perfect Echo] You understood the meaning in the repetition. rEx's emphasis mechanism is cracked.
